name: Portuguese Almond and Potato Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Bake or microwave the potato until soft. Remove the skin and force the pulp through a coarse sieve or potato ricer to obtain about 1 cup of lightly packed potato. Set aside to cool.
2. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C). Butter the sides of an 8½-inch spring form pan and line the bottom with baking parchment.
3. Using a nut mill, grind almonds to a fine powder and stir in baking powder. Set aside.
4. In a large bowl, beat egg whites until they start to stiffen. Gradually add sugar while continuing to beat until stiff and glossy.
5. Beat butter with remaining sugar, then beat in egg yolks, brandy, and almond extract. Using a rubber spatula, gently fold in the potato mixture, ground almonds, and egg whites.
6. Spoon the batter into the prepared pan and bake for 35-40 minutes or until a tester comes out clean. Let cool in the pan for 10 minutes.
7. Run a knife around the edge of the pan before releasing it. Turn the cake onto a rack to cool completely. Once cooled, carefully turn the cake right side up. Dust with powdered sugar and serve.
8. To make marzipan "potatoes," pinch off pieces of marzipan or almond paste and roll into elongated balls about the size of a large marble. Roll some in unsweetened cocoa powder to simulate brown potato skins. Arrange several groups on top of the cake and place others around the plate.
Marzipan "Potatoes" (4 ounces total):
- Marzipan or almond paste
- Unsweetened cocoa powder
Serves 10.
Per serving: 235 calories, 5g protein, 23g carbohydrate, 14g fat (5g saturated), 76mg cholesterol, 131mg sodium, 2g fiber.
